Title: City Keyword Search Returns All Matching Locations via GraphQL
Preconditions: QA backend accessible. Backend fix deployed. Address fields indexed in Elasticsearch.
Steps:
1. Execute productPickupLocations GraphQL query with keyword = "New York"
2. Record totalCount from response
3. Examine returned items for city field = "New York"
4. Compare totalCount against expected (30+)
Expected Result: totalCount >= 30. All returned items have address.city = New York. Response time < 2s. Before fix: 4 results.

Title: Postal Code Keyword Search Returns Matching Location via GraphQL
Preconditions: QA backend accessible. Backend fix deployed.
Steps:

2. Record totalCount

Expected Result: totalCount >= 1. Returned items have address.postalCode = 10059. Before fix: 0 results.

Expected Result: Both queries return all pickup locations. Counts are consistent.

Title: City Keyword Search is Case Insensitive
Preconditions: QA backend accessible. Backend fix deployed.
Steps:
1. Execute query with keyword = "new york"
2. Record totalCount
3. Execute with keyword = "NEW YORK"
4. Execute with keyword = "New York"
5. Compare counts
Expected Result: All three variants return same totalCount. Search is case-insensitive.

Title: Product Page BOPIS Modal - City Search Shows Correct Results
Preconditions: Logged in as test user. On BOPIS-eligible product page. Check pickup locations CTA visible.
Steps:
1. Navigate to FRONT_URL
2. Sign in with USER_EMAIL/USER_PASSWORD
3. Navigate to a product in Bolts category
4. Click "Check pickup locations" CTA
5. Note initial total count
6. Type "New York" in search field
7. Click Search
8. Record result count
9. Capture screenshot
Expected Result: Modal opens. Searching New York returns 30+ results (significantly more than old 4). Results list shows locations with New York in city/address.

Title: Cart BOPIS Modal Not Regressed by Indexing Changes
